items.title: Just how K-shaped is the U.S. economy? The top 10% spend nearly as much, excluding essentials, as bottom 70% combined
items.description: The gulf between the rich and everyone else is laid out in a stark analysis done by Bank of America’s economists on household spending.

items.title: ‘I make my own pizza.’ What’s the best way you’ve found to save money?
items.description: “Saving money doesn’t have to be about pizza — it could be coffee, cooking, cleaning, vacations, or anything else.”

items.title: ‘The money would have been life-changing’: My father told my grandmother to slash my inheritance. Is that fair?
items.description: “I could have bought a home for what I hope will be a growing family.”

items.title: SpaceX’s two lead underwriters have a $1 trillion chasm in their valuation as quiet period ends
items.description: The two lead underwriters on SpaceX’s initial public offering, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, have a valuation gap of more than $1 trillion as they both initiated coverage at buy.

items.title: Tough crowd for Samsung as record quarterly earnings brings out the sellers
items.description: Samsung Electronics shareholders proved a difficult audience to please.

items.title: Former ‘Grey’s Anatomy’ star Katherine Heigl is selling her gorgeous Utah home for $10.6 million
items.description: Katherine Heigl is selling her longtime Utah retreat 14 years after she left the Hollywood spotlight to enjoy a quieter life with her family at the extraordinary mountain home.

items.title: Dennis Quaid lists L.A. home for $5.2 million and makes the move to Nashville
items.description: The sun-soaked lifestyle he was able to enjoy at the home wasn’t enough to keep Quaid tethered to California, with the actor revealing recently that he made the decision to quit Los Angeles altogether

items.title: Trump calls Walmart ‘patriotic’ for cutting beef prices — in a stark change of tune
items.description: The president hasn’t always approved of Walmart’s moves, having criticized the chain’s response to tariffs last year.

items.title: The Nasdaq-100 has been far more volatile than the S&P 500. Now add SpaceX to the mix.
items.description: While SpaceX is due to join the Nasdaq-100 on Tuesday, it won’t become part of the S&P 500 for at least another year — likely furthering the volatility spread between those two indexes.

items.title: U.S.-Belgium match could draw record TV audience amid the Trump World Cup controversy
items.description: Some industry experts are predicting all-time viewing records for a U.S. soccer game
